{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Alekhine's Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |nd|pl| | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} = Alekhine's Defence = With his knight finally safe, Black can start to put pressure on White's pawn center. The most aggressive move in this position is [[/3...d6/]] or [[/3...e6|e6]]. Black also has the option of playing the dubious but somewhat tricky O'Sullivan Gambit with [[/3...b5/]]. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}. '1.e4 Nf6 2.e5 Nd5 3.d4'
